edges.description: Reduced VPS13D function disrupts bulk lipid transfer at organelle membrane contact sites and the autophagic/mitochondrial size-control and clearance processes that depend on it.
edges.target: Impaired Lipid Transfer and Mitochondrial Quality Control

edges.description: Disrupted lipid transfer and mitochondrial quality control produce abnormal mitochondrial morphology and impaired axonal distribution, leading to bioenergetic failure.
edges.source: Impaired Lipid Transfer and Mitochondrial Quality Control

edges.description: Mitochondrial dysfunction compromises cerebellar and brainstem neurons that control coordination and ocular fixation, producing ataxia and saccadic intrusions.
edges.target: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

edges.description: The same bioenergetic lesion injures corticospinal tract and long peripheral axons, producing spasticity and axonal sensorimotor neuropathy.
Cerebellar circuit dysfunction produces progressive cerebellar ataxia.
edges.source: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

edges.description: Brainstem and cerebellar oculomotor circuit dysfunction disrupts steady fixation.
edges.source: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

edges.description: Delayed cerebellar feedback control produces macrosaccadic oscillations.
edges.source: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

edges.description: Cerebellar saccadic dysmetria produces overshooting hypermetric saccades.
edges.source: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

edges.description: Cerebellar and related motor-circuit dysfunction can contribute to the severe tremor reported in the VPS13D-related spectrum.
edges.source: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

edges.description: Degeneration of long peripheral axons produces axonal sensorimotor neuropathy.

edges.target: Impaired Lipid Transfer and Mitochondrial Quality Control

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in VPS13D (1p36) impair VPS13D protein function, typically through one loss-of-function (nonsense/splice) allele combined with a milder missense or non-canonical splice allele. This is the upstream genetic lesion that initiates all downstream pathophysiology.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: Exome sequencing identified compound heterozygous mutations in VPS13D on chromosome 1p36 in all 7 families.

nodes.description: Dysfunction of cerebellar circuitry (including Purkinje cells and the cerebellar control of saccades) and brainstem oculomotor networks disrupts coordination and steady ocular fixation. The result is cerebellar ataxia together with saccadic intrusions such as macrosaccadic oscillations and hypermetric (overshooting) saccades; smooth pursuit and vestibular function may be relatively spared. Delayed feedback control from slowed conduction in cerebellar parallel fibers has been proposed to explain the saccadic disorder.
nodes.id: Cerebellar and Brainstem Oculomotor Circuit Dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: Affected patients showed overshooting horizontal saccades, macrosaccadic oscillations, and increased velocity of larger saccades; other eye movements were normal.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: the saccadic disorder, which would be caused by delayed feedback control because of slow conduction in cerebellar parallel fibers.

nodes.description: Progressive cerebellar ataxia is the core, near-universal manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: The phenotypic spectrum in our 12 patients was broad. Although most presented with ataxia, additional or predominant spasticity was present in 5 patients.

nodes.description: Involvement of the corticospinal (pyramidal) system produces spasticity and pyramidal signs, while axonal degeneration of long peripheral nerves causes a sensorimotor neuropathy. Slowed conduction in selectively vulnerable axons is implicated.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: We describe a family of Slovenian descent with progressive ataxia, corticospinal signs, axonal sensorimotor neuropathy, and disruption of visual fixation by saccadic intrusions.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: Although most presented with ataxia, additional or predominant spasticity was present in 5 patients.

nodes.description: Overshooting (hypermetric) saccades reflecting cerebellar saccadic dysmetria.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: Affected patients showed overshooting horizontal saccades, macrosaccadic oscillations, and increased velocity of larger saccades; other eye movements were normal.

nodes.description: VPS13D is a bulk lipid transporter at membrane contact sites required for mitochondrial size control, distribution, and clearance (mitophagy/ autophagy). Its loss disrupts intermembrane lipid transfer and mitochondrial organization, the common molecular lesion that feeds downstream mitochondrial dysfunction.
nodes.id: Impaired Lipid Transfer and Mitochondrial Quality Control

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: Knockdown or removal of Vps13D in Drosophila neurons led to changes in mitochondrial morphology and impairment in mitochondrial distribution along axons.

nodes.description: Large back-to-back saccades oscillating around the fixation target.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: Affected patients showed overshooting horizontal saccades, macrosaccadic oscillations, and increased velocity of larger saccades; other eye movements were normal.

nodes.description: VPS13D-deficient cells exhibit abnormal mitochondrial morphology (enlarged/rounded mitochondria, perinuclear accumulation), impaired axonal mitochondrial distribution, and reduced oxidative energy production. Neurons with high metabolic and axonal-transport demands are selectively vulnerable.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: Patient fibroblasts showed altered morphology and functionality including reduced energy production.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: progressive ataxia, corticospinal signs, axonal sensorimotor neuropathy, and disruption of visual fixation by saccadic intrusions.

nodes.description: Disruption of steady visual fixation by saccadic intrusions, including macrosaccadic oscillations and overshooting/hypermetric saccades, is a defining oculomotor feature.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of clinical signs in recessive ataxia with saccadic intrusions.
evidence.snippet: progressive ataxia, corticospinal signs, axonal sensorimotor neuropathy, and disruption of visual fixation by saccadic intrusions.

nodes.description: Pyramidal/corticospinal involvement with spasticity, predominant in a subset.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: Although most presented with ataxia, additional or predominant spasticity was present in 5 patients.

nodes.description: Tremor occurs in a subset of patients with VPS13D-related disorder and can be severe/debilitating.

evidence.reference_title: Not to Miss: Intronic Variants, Treatment, and Review of the Phenotypic Spectrum in VPS13D-Related Disorder.
evidence.snippet: one patient with early onset severe spastic ataxia and debilitating tremor

nodes.description: Nonsense (loss-of-function) VPS13D allele reported in the SCASI/SCAR4 family.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in VPS13D lead to a new recessive ataxia with spasticity and mitochondrial defects.
evidence.snippet: All but 2 patients carried a loss-of-function (nonsense or splice site) mutation on one and a missense mutation on the other allele.
